meta.our_score_projection_note: Elitez Events scores reflect 12-month buildout target (2026-Q2 to 2027-Q2): pricing calculator launches 2026-Q3, AI-event features 2026-Q4, ISO 20121 audit by 2027-Q1. Today's baseline: published_pricing 2, ai_enabled_production 1, esg_certification 2, hybrid_livestream 3.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez breaks the Red Ocean cluster on ecosystem_manpower_depth (in-house promoter + temp staffing + security arm — zero competitors match) and published_pricing_transparency (25 of 35 still hide rate cards), at the deliberate cost of creative_theming_originality where Cohesion's Bumball IP and Visionnaire's luxury craft will continue to win the boutique brief.

rubrics.published_pricing_transparency: 0 = no price anywhere on site, full quote-on-brief. 1 = vague 'budget starts from' phrase, no per-pax. 2 = partial cost guide blog post but no SKU pricing. 3 = published per-pax band or per-station list price. 4 = published tier pricing with what-in/what-out. 5 = self-serve calculator with PAX × tier × add-ons returning a 24h budget band.
rubrics.ecosystem_manpower_depth: 0 = pure agency model, 100% subcontracted crew. 1 = retained freelance MC/photographer pool only. 2 = small in-house production crew (5-10 people). 3 = in-house creative + production crew but subcontracts promoters/security. 4 = in-house promoter pool + production crew, security via partner. 5 = in-house promoter pool + temp staffing arm + security arm + payroll/levy compliance under one group P&L.
rubrics.esg_certification: 0 = no ESG mention. 1 = sustainability claims, no audit. 2 = sustainability positioning + supplier checklists. 3 = single-event carbon report on request. 4 = ISO 20121 OR EcoVadis (any colour). 5 = ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold + carbon-tracking disclosed per event.
rubrics.ai_enabled_production: 0 = no AI features. 1 = generic 'AI-friendly' marketing copy. 2 = one AI tool (e.g. AI photobooth) bolted on. 3 = AI photobooth + RSVP triage assistant. 4 = AI photobooth + RSVP triage + post-event sentiment dashboard + attendee personalisation. 5 = full AI-event stack + IMDA pre-approved vendor (PSG/EDG GenAI grant track).
rubrics.hybrid_livestream_capability: 0 = no hybrid offering. 1 = basic Zoom relay subcontracted. 2 = single-camera livestream via partner. 3 = 2-camera livestream-ready in-house. 4 = 4-camera switcher feed + hybrid studio + captioning in-house. 5 = full broadcast-grade studio + AR/VR experiential + multi-room hybrid + 270-degree projection.
rubrics.govt_tender_credentials: 0 = no public-sector wins. 1 = single stat-board client. 2 = handful of public-sector references. 3 = multiple stat-board / healthcare cluster clients on case-study page. 4 = PCO award (Stella, ICCA) AND multi-year framework with stat board / cluster. 5 = Best PCO Asia (Stella) + multiple multi-year GeBIZ framework agreements + Minister-grade event experience.
rubrics.creative_theming_originality: 0 = template-only execution. 1 = generic theme library, no in-house design. 2 = in-house designer producing standard themes. 3 = in-house creative team + custom theme storyboards. 4 = bespoke creative with mood films + branded show-call scripts. 5 = proprietary game/show IP (e.g. Bumball) OR luxury creative pedigree with global awards.
rubrics.regional_rollout_reach: 0 = SG-only. 1 = SG + 1 SEA neighbour ad-hoc. 2 = SG + 2-3 SEA ad-hoc partners. 3 = formal SEA partner network across 4+ countries. 4 = own offices in 3+ SEA countries OR established regional fulfilment muscle. 5 = own offices in 6+ countries / 30+ offices globally with cross-border roll-out muscle.

attack_plans.icp: Brand / trade-marketing manager at FMCG, consumer-tech, automotive or pharma brand running 4-12 week roving SG roadshow / sampling / pop-up programme requiring 8-15 promoters per shift across 3-6 mall / MRT / HDB sites — currently splits creative agency + 2-3 staffing agencies and personally coordinates the seam.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: From SAM SGD 360M (Elitez serviceable band per market-intelligence.json), brand activation = 18% of category mix per pricing-strategy.json brand-marketing economic signal (retail sales +6.3-8.3% YoY) = SGD ~65M serviceable. Filter to manpower-heavy roving formats (excludes single-venue launch events): ~75% = SGD 48M annual TAM. Cross-check: SG has ~400 active FMCG/consumer/auto brand teams running >=2 activations/yr at avg SGD 80,000 expected per Brand Activation Marketer persona (pricing-strategy.json) — 400 brands x 1.5 activations/yr x SGD 80,000 = SGD 48M. Bottom-up sanity check matches.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows 0 competitors scoring >=3 on brand_activation_marketer x bundled_manpower. The structural reason: SG agencies are either creative-led (INX, Visionnaire, Twist Media, Mileage) and subcontract crew at +30%, or staffing-led (Adecco, Recruit Express) with no creative capability. No dataset competitor owns BOTH creative and an in-house promoter pool + temp-staffing arm + security stack under one P&L. Pico has cross-border crew but not SG promoter pool; Slite has fabrication but not promoter manpower; Cityneon has global crew but enterprise pricing. The structural moat: building an in-house promoter pool is a 5+ year HR-business buildout — Elitez's parent already has it, no competitor can replicate inside the 12-month window.
attack_plans.why_we_win: ecosystem_manpower_depth: 5/5 (only score in entire dataset alongside us). The Elitez Group HR/staffing/security arm provides 800+ promoter-shifts/yr at 7-day recall under one payroll, one MOM compliance file, one PDPA agreement. This collapses the brand manager's vendor count from 4 (creative + 3 staffing) to 1, recovers the 30-50% staffing-agency mark-up they currently lose, and absorbs the 2025-09 S Pass + 2026-07 LQS wage-floor inflation that solo agencies cannot. Bespoke Retainer tier (SGD 400,000/yr per pricing-strategy.json) is built around this exact play.

gtm.channel: SG Marketing Society + AdAsia + LIA-SG community sponsorships; targeted LinkedIn outbound to 'Trade Marketing Manager' / 'Brand Activation Lead' titles at top-50 SG-resident FMCG/consumer/auto brands; quarterly 'manpower-quality benchmark' breakfast for marketing heads; partner referrals from venue leasing teams (CapitaLand, Frasers, Mapletree mall management).
gtm.pitch: One PO. One creative team. One in-house promoter pool that won't no-show. Recover the 30% you currently lose to staffing-agency mark-ups while we absorb 2026's S Pass + LQS hikes for you.
gtm.pricing: Bespoke Retainer SGD 400,000/yr (pricing-strategy.json) covering 1 flagship + 4-6 activations + 800 promoter-shifts; or per-activation Standard at SGD 80,000-220,000 (Brand Activation Marketer persona WTP band). EDG-eligible when scoped as brand-experience capability project — net SGD 360,000.
gtm.content: (1) 'Manpower TCO Teardown' — public benchmark of split-brief (creative + 3 staffing agencies) vs Elitez bundle, quantifying the SGD 24,000 mark-up recovery on a SGD 80,000 activation. (2) Case study with 1 FMCG brand showing day-of attendance rate >97% across 6 sites vs industry 88%. (3) Ungated whitepaper 'The 2026 SG Manpower Cost Cliff: S Pass + LQS impact on activation budgets'. (4) 90-second highlight reel of Elitez Group HR/security/promoter stack delivering one activation end-to-end. (5) Procurement-grade one-pager on PDPA + MOM compliance under one consolidated agreement.

attack_plans.icp: HR / Internal Comms manager (Annual D&D Coordinator persona) at SG corporate 200-1,500 staff scoping the annual Q4 D&D, frustrated by the 25-of-35 'contact us' competitor wall — currently writes a Word brief, sends to 3-4 incumbents, waits 2-4 weeks for proposals to compare, then defends the per-pax line to a CFO citing 'just book MBS direct'.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: From SAM SGD 360M, the Annual D&D Coordinator slice (200-1,500 PAX D&D market — pricing-strategy.json) = ~35% of the category = SGD 126M. Of those, ~75% currently route through hidden-pricing agency RFP (25/35 competitors hide rate cards per cultural_signals.json) = SGD 95M is the addressable opacity-arbitrage flow. Cross-check: ~1,200 SG mid-market employers run annual D&D at avg SGD 80,000 contracts (Annual D&D Coordinator expected WTP) = SGD 96M. Numbers triangulate.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows only 1 competitor (Get Out! Events with 2026 cost guide) scoring 3 on annual_dnd_coordinator x published_pricing — and Get Out's 'guide' is still not a self-serve calculator returning a 24h budget band. DIY-hotel-banquet routes score higher but are not agency offerings. The structural reason competitors hide: they fear price-anchored procurement teams will benchmark them down, and they protect 12-20% management-fee margin through opacity. Cracking opacity requires committing to defensible escalator clauses and trusting that conversion-rate uplift outweighs margin compression — a strategic call all 25 hidden-price competitors have collectively avoided. Cultural_signals.json flags this as the timed wedge cracking 2026-Q4.
attack_plans.why_we_win: published_pricing_transparency: 4/5 projection (from baseline 2). Elitez Standard D&D tier published at SGD 50,000 (pricing-strategy.json) with explicit what-in / what-out, plus PSG-eligible net SGD 35,000. Self-serve calculator (PAX x tier x add-ons) ships 2026-Q3 per market-intelligence trends action window. Combined with the Standard tier 'from SGD 130/pax' anchor — the only mid-market agency price meeting the buyer mental model that already calibrates against MBS/RWS SGD 100-200/pax. Defensible because: (a) Elitez Group manpower cost moat lets us hold price even when wage floor lifts, (b) pre-blocked Q4 ballroom inventory removes the 'venue not available' escape, (c) PSG pre-approval listing locks the SGD 35,000 net anchor on the buyer's procurement portal.

gtm.channel: Public Elitez Events pricing calculator + PSG pre-approved listing page on IMDA portal as the dominant inbound mechanism; SBF + SHRI HR-community partnerships (D&D-season webinars Apr-Jul); SEO-targeted long-form ('SG D&D pricing 2026 — published, not contact-us') ranking against the 25 hidden competitors; LinkedIn outbound to 'HR Director / Internal Comms Lead' at SG-resident 200-1,500 employers in May-Aug front-loaded for Q4 booking peak.
gtm.pitch: Stop chasing 4 'contact us' agencies for 3 weeks. Get a SGD-banded D&D quote in 60 seconds, PSG-discounted, with pre-blocked Q4 ballroom inventory. We publish; they hide.
gtm.pricing: Standard D&D SGD 50,000 list (pricing-strategy.json) / SGD 35,000 net post-PSG (50% / cap SGD 30k) for 300-600 PAX; SGD 130/pax anchor; what-in includes show-called programme + Elitez ecosystem manpower + livestream-ready capture + 2-photographer photography stack. Calculator returns SGD-banded quote in <60s.
gtm.content: (1) Public pricing calculator (PAX x tier x add-ons -> 24h budget band) on elitez-events.com homepage. (2) 'The 71% Wall' interactive comparison tool listing all 25 hidden-price competitors next to Elitez published rate. (3) PSG application walkthrough (3-min video) targeted at HR who haven't claimed PSG before. (4) Q3 D&D-season '2026 Pricing Index' annual report for download (gated lead-gen). (5) SHRI / SBF guest article on 'Why opacity costs HR teams 4 weeks of procurement runway'.

attack_plans.niche_name: ISO 20121 events for ESG-screened GLC RFPs (2027-Q2)
attack_plans.icp: Procurement / sustainability lead at SG-listed corporate, GLC, or stat-board (companies subject to SGX climate-disclosure phasing 2025-2027 — DBS, OCBC, Singtel, Singapore Airlines, CapitaLand, Sembcorp, ST Engineering, Keppel, GovTech, EDB, MOH-clusters) running events whose RFP scoresheet now requires ESG attestation as pass/fail by 2027-Q2.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: From SAM SGD 360M, ESG-screened GLC + listed-corporate slice = ~20% of the category by event-spend share (top-100 SGX-listed + 40 GLCs + 60 stat boards × annual events). 140 entities × avg SGD 200k annual event spend (skews higher due to size) × 75% addressable for sustainability-screened scope = SGD 21M Year 1 (2026); rises to SGD 72M annualised by 2028 once SGX climate-disclosure phasing covers all primary-listed and GLCs deepen ESG procurement rules. Triangulation: Visionnaire's premium niche today (~SGD 8M revenue per ACRA estimate) = ~10% of the addressable flow proves the segment exists at audit-ready price points.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows 1 competitor (Visionnaire at score 5 on iso_20121_esg, sole holder of ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold) scoring >=3 on esg_screened_glc x iso_20121_esg. Funco's sustainability positioning is not certification-grade; Pico's group-level disclosures are investor-facing not event-specific. The structural reason: ISO 20121 audit is a 12-18 month commitment with SGD 30-80k initial cost + ongoing surveillance audits — capital-intensive for the bottom-30 boutique competitors (Mariko, Mega, JNR, Electric Dreamz) and culturally misaligned for the show-called-D&D shops. Visionnaire's 17-year head start makes them today's only credible answer; market-intelligence trends flag 2027-Q2 as the procurement flip date when ESG attestation moves from differentiator to gate.
attack_plans.why_we_win: esg_certification: today 2/5, projection 4/5 by 2027-Q1 once Elitez completes ISO 20121 application by 2026-Q4. Defensible reasoning: (a) Elitez Group corporate maturity supports the surveillance-audit commitment that SG boutique competitors cannot afford; (b) ecosystem manpower moat lets us track Scope 3 supplier-emissions data already through internal HR-payroll system, the hardest part of ISO 20121 reporting; (c) WDG(JR+) grant subsidises 70% of the consultant cost to redesign internal workflows to meet ISO 20121 requirements — turns the audit into a grant-funded capability project. Combined with our_score 4 on bundled_manpower (single-PO ESG-attested supply chain) and 4 on hybrid_broadcast (matching MCI/Pico for AGM-grade ESG conferences), we credibly displace Visionnaire on price (Visionnaire is luxury-niche premium-priced) at the mid-market 200-1,500 PAX layer where they don't play.

gtm.channel: SACEOS membership upgrade (2026-Q3) for MICE Sustainability Roadmap channel access; SGX-listed company secretarial network outbound (SID, SIAS); Climate Governance Forum annual sponsorship (SGX RegCo / ACRA / SID co-organisers); ISO 20121 announcement campaign timed to certification (2027-Q1) with SGTech + SBF press syndicate; STB BEiS sustainability priority track for exhibition-support work.
gtm.pitch: When your 2027 sustainability-screened RFP lands, the only ISO 20121 + EcoVadis-credentialed agency on it costs 60% more than us — and we run the manpower and broadcast hybrid stack the boutique can't. Same audit, half the gala bill.
gtm.pricing: Premium Themed Gala SGD 250,000 list / EDG-bundleable to net SGD 75,000-125,000 (pricing-strategy.json) when scoped as 'sustainable event capability project'. ESG green-tier add-on at +8-12% drawn from CSR / ESG budget envelope (different procurement gate per cultural_signals.json). Bespoke Retainer SGD 400,000 for annual GLC/listco programme with quarterly ESG reporting dashboard.
gtm.content: (1) ISO 20121 application milestone tracker (public-facing transparency lever) — 'X weeks to audit'. (2) White paper 'SGX Climate-Disclosure 2025-2027: What event-supplier ESG attestation will be required' co-authored with SACEOS sustainability committee. (3) Carbon-tracking dashboard demo (per-event Scope 1/2/3) for procurement teams to inspect. (4) Visionnaire-comparison teardown anchored on price-per-PAX showing 40-50% savings for equivalent ISO-attested scope at 200-1,500 PAX scale. (5) WDG(JR+) bundled offer for GLC clients — 70% grant-funded engagement-team workflow redesign alongside event delivery.

attack_plans.niche_name: AI-enabled hybrid production for IMDA GenAI grant
attack_plans.icp: Annual D&D Coordinator persona at innovation-forward SG corporate (200-1,500 staff at tech, finance, healthcare-tech) and Brand Activation Marketer at consumer-tech / FMCG brand running activations where 'AI-personalised attendee experience' is appearing in 2026 RFP language — currently has no budget line for AI features but the procurement scoresheet is increasingly weighting it.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: From SAM SGD 360M, the AI-feature-attach addressable flow is ~8% in 2026 (early-adopter share per market-intelligence trends) = SGD 29M. Filtered to D&D coordinators + brand-activation marketers in tech-forward verticals (Nadq SG-listed + tech MNCs + healthtech + fintech ~350 entities × avg SGD 80k annual event with AI add-on at 10% premium) = SGD 28M. Conservative against MCI's '350+ AI-driven activations 2024' benchmark which proves volume exists at premium tier.
attack_plans.why_gap: Heatmap shows 1 competitor at score >=4 on annual_dnd_coordinator x ai_event_features (MCI Group SG at 4) and 1 (Tembusu Events at 3) on AR/VR-flavoured AI. The structural reason this is green: (a) MCI's '350+ AI activations' comes from Geneva-led R&D pricing at network-agency tier — overkill for SG mid-market D&D; (b) Tembusu's AR/VR is feature-list-positioned, not buyer-jobs-to-be-done; (c) no SG corporate has a budget line for 'AI in our D&D' yet (per cultural_signals.json) so competitors haven't invested. Trends flag IMDA's expected 2027-Q1 GenAI SME grant as the demand catalyst — first agency on the pre-approved vendor list captures inbound.
attack_plans.why_we_win: ai_enabled_production: today 1/5, projection 3/5 by 2026-Q4 once Elitez ships AI photobooth + RSVP triage + post-event sentiment dashboard, projection 5/5 by 2027-Q2 once on IMDA pre-approved list. Defensible because: (a) action-window timing — shipping AI features by 2026-Q4 puts us first on IMDA's approval pipeline when criteria publish; (b) Elitez Group's adjacent tech investments (XinceAI workflow stack per parent company portfolio) provide engineering bench without third-party tooling cost; (c) bundling AI features into Plus/Premium tier rather than charging premium SKU avoids the 'no budget line yet' purchase friction other agencies hit. Win = MCI's premium positioning compressed to mid-market price + grant subsidy = irresistible.

gtm.channel: IMDA / SGTech AI-event grant pre-approval pipeline (apply 2026-Q4 ahead of 2027-Q1 announcement); SACEOS Singapore MICE Forum 2026 'AI in Events' track sponsorship; targeted demo-day series for HR / brand teams at NUS-ISS, SGTech AI Council, Lazada/Shopee/Grab tech communities; partnership with Singapore AI Verify Foundation for procurement-grade AI-ethics attestation overlay.
gtm.pitch: Same D&D, except guests get AI-personalised seating, GenAI photobooth keepsakes, and your HR team gets a sentiment dashboard by Monday morning. PSG-funded the moment IMDA opens the GenAI track in 2027-Q1. Beat MCI's enterprise tier at half the price.
gtm.pricing: Bundled inside Standard D&D SGD 50,000 (no premium SKU in 2026 per cultural_signals AI-experiment phase); standalone AI-Tier add-on SGD 8,000-15,000 launches 2027-Q1 tied to IMDA grant announcement. PSG-eligible at 50% (cap SGD 30k) once IMDA-listed.
gtm.content: (1) Live demo deck 'GenAI photobooth + RSVP triage in 90 seconds' rotating at SACEOS 2026 forum. (2) IMDA pre-approval submission status tracker on elitez-events.com (public timeline). (3) Three case studies of 2026-Q4 D&D launches with AI features showing +18% RSVP-confirmation rate, +24% same-day photo-share, post-event NPS dashboard. (4) Side-by-side teardown vs MCI Group SG's enterprise AI-event pricing (SGD 12k Elitez add-on vs SGD 60k MCI module). (5) AI-ethics attestation page (AI Verify Foundation alignment) for procurement teams.
